intro: Terebinthina javanica, also known as the Java frankincense, is a plant with resinous properties, similar to frankincense, obtained from the Boswellia serrata or Boswellia dalzielii tree. The health benefits of Terebinthina javanica include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, which can aid in relieving pain and swelling, as well as promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include boswellic acids, specifically acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA), which has been shown to exhibit potent anti-inflammatory activity. Terebinthina javanica is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as topical creams and ointments, as well as in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine formulations.
